vocabulary.definition_ar: قهوة تُصنع بنقع القهوة المطحونة الخشنة في الماء البارد لساعات عديدة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Cold brew coffee is less acidic than hot brewed coffee.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طبقة الرغوة الذهبية التي تتكون على سطح الإسبريسو المستخلص بشكل صحيح
vocabulary.example_sentence: Good espresso should have a thick, lasting crema on top.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة مهنية لتذوق القهوة لتقييم جودتها ونكهتها
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coffee cupping helps buyers determine which beans to purchase.

vocabulary.definition_ar: جودة امتلاك نكهات وخصائص متعددة ومختلفة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The complexity of this Ethiopian coffee makes every sip a new experience.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الإطلاق الطبيعي لثاني أكسيد الكربون من حبوب القهوة المحمصة حديثاً
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coffee needs degassing time after roasting for optimal brewing results.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مشروب قهوة إسباني مصنوع من أجزاء متساوية من الإسبريسو والحليب الدافئ
vocabulary.example_sentence: The cortado is served in a small glass cup.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة لتجفيف ثمار القهوة تحت الشمس قبل إزالة الحبوب
vocabulary.example_sentence: Dry process coffees typically have fuller body and fruity flavors.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نظام تقييم رقمي يُستخدم لتقييم جودة القهوة مهنياً
vocabulary.example_sentence: Specialty coffees typically have cup scores above eighty points.

vocabulary.definition_ar: صوت الفرقعة المسموع الذي تصدره حبوب القهوة أثناء التحميص الخفيف إلى المتوسط
vocabulary.example_sentence: First crack indicates that the coffee beans are ready for light roasting.

vocabulary.definition_ar: إبريق قهوة عربي تقليدي له فوهة طويلة يُستخدم لتقديم القهوة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The host filled the dallah with qahwa before welcoming his guests.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يمتلك أو يظهر حكماً جيداً والقدرة على ملاحظة الفروق الدقيقة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Discerning coffee drinkers can taste the difference between various brewing methods.

vocabulary.definition_ar: رائحة محببة ورقيقة، خاصةً من القهوة أو الطعام
vocabulary.example_sentence: The fragrance of the freshly ground beans filled the small café.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ذو طعم أو رائحة يذكّرك بالتراب أو الأرض
vocabulary.example_sentence: Indonesian coffees often have a bold, earthy flavor that some people love.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة معالجة القهوة بترك بعض لب الثمرة على الحبوب أثناء التجفيف
vocabulary.example_sentence: Honey processed coffee combines characteristics of wet and dry methods.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مشروب إسبريسو مغطى بكمية صغيرة من رغوة الحليب المبخرة
vocabulary.example_sentence: A traditional macchiato is much smaller than modern coffee shop versions.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ذو طعم أو رائحة تشبه الزهور
vocabulary.example_sentence: Light roasted Ethiopian coffees are known for their delicate floral notes.

vocabulary.definition_ar: جهاز تحضير القهوة الذي يستخدم مكبساً شبكياً معدنياً لفصل القهوة المطحونة
vocabulary.example_sentence: French press coffee has a full body and rich flavor.

vocabulary.definition_ar: آلة تحضير قهوة على الموقد تدفع البخار عبر القهوة المطحونة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The moka pot produces a strong, concentrated coffee without an espresso machine.

vocabulary.definition_ar: قهوة باردة مُحقنة بغاز النيتروجين للحصول على قوام كريمي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Nitro coffee is served on tap like beer in many cafes.

vocabulary.definition_ar: عملية إزالة الطبقة الورقية من حبوب القهوة المجففة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Hulling is one of the final steps before coffee beans are sorted.

vocabulary.definition_ar: دفعة صغيرة من القهوة من قسم محدد في المزرعة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Micro lot coffees often have unique and distinctive flavor profiles.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الطبقة الورقية التي تغطي حبوب القهوة بعد المعالجة الأولية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coffee beans are stored in parchment before final hulling and sorting.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الطبقة الخارجية اللحمية الطرية لثمرة البن التي تحيط بالحبة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The pulp is removed during wet processing to reveal the parchment layer.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الإحساس الجسدي والملمس للقهوة في الفم أثناء الشرب
vocabulary.example_sentence: This coffee has a smooth mouthfeel with medium body weight.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نوع من القهوة ذو طعم أقوى ومحتوى كافيين أعلى من الأرابيكا
vocabulary.example_sentence: Robusta coffee is often used in instant coffee blends.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ذو طعم أو رائحة تشبه المكسرات
vocabulary.example_sentence: Brazilian coffees are popular for their smooth, nutty, and chocolatey character.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة تحضير يدوية حيث يُسكب الماء الساخن ببطء على القهوة المطحونة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Pour over brewing gives you more control over extraction time.

vocabulary.definition_ar: جعل شيء مختلفاً أو مميزاً مقارنة بالآخرين
vocabulary.example_sentence: The unique brewing method sets this coffee shop apart from its competitors.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يُؤدى كجزء من طقوس أو وفقاً لعادة راسخة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Morning coffee preparation became a ritualistic practice for many people.

vocabulary.definition_ar: قهوة من مصدر واحد محدد أو مزرعة واحدة بدلاً من الخلط
vocabulary.example_sentence: Single origin coffee allows you to taste the unique regional characteristics.

vocabulary.definition_ar: صفة التهذيب والثقافة أو امتلاك معرفة وخبرة واسعة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The coffee shop's sophistication attracted customers who appreciated quality drinks.

vocabulary.definition_ar: صوت طقطقة أهدأ أثناء مراحل التحميص الداكن لحبوب القهوة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Second crack begins when coffee reaches the dark roast level.

vocabulary.definition_ar: كلمات وصفية تُستخدم لتوصيف النكهات والروائح في القهوة
vocabulary.example_sentence: This coffee's tasting notes include chocolate, caramel, and citrus hints.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الغشاء الرقيق الذي يبقى على حبوب القهوة بعد التقشير
vocabulary.example_sentence: Most silverskin comes off during roasting, creating chaff.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة تحضير تستخدم غرفتين وضغطًا تفريغيًا لإنتاج قهوة صافية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Siphon coffee produces a clean, tea-like cup with a delicate flavor.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حركة تتعامل مع القهوة كحرفة فنية وليس كسلعة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Third wave coffee shops focus on quality and brewing methods.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة لإزالة الثمرة من حبوب القهوة باستخدام الماء والتخمير
vocabulary.example_sentence: Wet processed coffee often has brighter, cleaner flavors than dry processed.

vocabulary.definition_ar: النشأة أو التطور من مصدر معين
vocabulary.example_sentence: The global love of coffee is thought to stem from ancient Ethiopian traditions.

vocabulary.definition_ar: العوامل البيئية التي تؤثر على طعم القهوة، بما في ذلك المناخ والتربة
vocabulary.example_sentence: The terroir of Colombian mountains produces distinctive coffee flavors.

vocabulary.definition_ar: موجود أو ظاهر أو متواجد في كل مكان في نفس الوقت
vocabulary.example_sentence: Coffee shops have become ubiquitous in modern cities around the world.

paragraphs: Coffee has *evolved* from a regional Ethiopian discovery into one of the world's most *influential* beverages, shaping social customs and economic systems across diverse cultures. Today, this humble bean connects millions of people daily through shared rituals that *transcend* geographical boundaries and cultural differences.

The story begins in ancient Ethiopia, where legend claims a goat herder first observed his animals becoming energetic after eating certain berries. From these modest origins, coffee *spread throughout* the Ottoman Empire and eventually reached Europe in the 17th century. European traders were initially *skeptical* about this dark, bitter drink, but they soon recognized its commercial potential. Coffee houses quickly became *vibrant* social centers where intellectuals, merchants, and artists would *gather* to exchange ideas and conduct business.

Different regions have developed their own *distinctive* coffee traditions that reflect local values and lifestyles. In Italy, the espresso culture *emphasizes* quick, standing consumption at neighborhood bars, fostering brief but meaningful social interactions. Scandinavian countries have *embraced* the concept of 'fika' – a coffee break that prioritizes relaxation and community bonding over productivity. Meanwhile, Ethiopian coffee ceremonies involve elaborate preparation rituals that can last several hours, demonstrating how coffee serves as a *foundation* for family gatherings and hospitality.

The global coffee industry has *undergone* significant transformation in recent decades, with specialty coffee movements gaining momentum worldwide. Modern consumers are increasingly *conscious* of ethical sourcing practices and environmental sustainability. Third-wave coffee culture treats coffee preparation as an art form, where baristas *demonstrate* technical expertise similar to skilled craftspeople. This evolution has created new opportunities for coffee-producing countries to *participate* more profitably in the global market, moving beyond simple commodity trading to value-added processing and branding.

Today's coffee culture continues to adapt to changing social needs and technological advances. Whether it's a traditional Arabic qahwa shared among friends, a carefully crafted pour-over in a trendy café, or a quick espresso grabbed during a busy workday, coffee remains a universal language that brings people together across cultures, generations, and social boundaries.

passages.label: B
passages.title: Coffee's Journey from Bean to Cup

paragraphs: When you *savor* your morning coffee, you're experiencing the result of a complex journey that began thousands of miles away in the mountains of Ethiopia, Colombia, or Brazil. The path from coffee bean to your cup involves careful *cultivation*, precise processing, and an intricate global supply chain that connects farmers in remote villages to consumers in bustling cities worldwide.

Coffee plants are remarkably *sensitive* to their environment, thriving only in specific regions known as the 'coffee belt' – a narrow band around the equator between 25 degrees north and 30 degrees south latitude. Within this belt, altitude plays a *crucial* role in determining coffee quality. Beans grown at higher elevations, typically between 1,200 and 2,000 meters above sea level, develop more slowly and *acquire* complex flavors that coffee experts highly prize. It has been reported that Ethiopian coffee farmers, who *maintain* traditional growing methods passed down through generations, can distinguish between beans from different hillsides simply by taste.

The processing methods employed after harvest significantly *influence* the final flavor profile of coffee beans. The wet processing method, which is *prevalent* in Central America, involves removing the fruit pulp immediately after picking and fermenting the beans in water for 12-48 hours. This technique typically produces coffee with bright acidity and clean flavors. In contrast, the dry processing method, commonly used in Ethiopia and Brazil, allows the entire coffee cherry to dry in the sun before the bean is extracted. If farmers *adhere* to proper drying techniques, this method can create coffee with fuller body and fruity characteristics.

Climate change has begun to *disrupt* traditional coffee-growing regions, forcing farmers to adapt their practices or relocate to higher altitudes. Scientists predict that by 2050, the area suitable for coffee cultivation could decrease by up to 50%. However, innovative farmers are learning to *cope* with these challenges by developing drought-resistant varieties and implementing sustainable farming practices. Many coffee cooperatives now *participate* in certification programs that promote environmental protection while ensuring fair prices for farmers.

The global coffee trade represents a fascinating example of how local agricultural practices connect to international markets. When specialty coffee roasters *acquire* beans directly from specific farms through 'direct trade' relationships, they often pay premium prices that help farming communities invest in better equipment and education. This approach has proven more *beneficial* than traditional commodity trading, as it allows farmers to capture more value from their crops while giving consumers access to exceptional coffee with traceable origins. Research indicates that direct trade relationships can increase farmer income by 200-300% compared to conventional supply chains.

sections.content_ar: الصوت المبني للمجهول يركز على الفعل أو متلقي الفعل، وليس على فاعل الفعل. نستخدمه عندما يكون الفاعل غير معروف أو غير مهم، أو عندما نريد التركيز على النتيجة أو العملية.
sections.content_en:
Passive Voice (Present)

Look at these examples:
• Coffee is grown in Ethiopia.
• Espresso machines are used in Italian cafés.
• Turkish coffee is prepared with special techniques.

In passive voice, we focus on the action or the receiver of the action, not who does it. We use passive voice when:
• The doer is unknown or not important
• We want to focus on the result/process
• In formal writing or scientific descriptions

Formation: Subject + am/is/are + past participle

The past participle is usually the same as the past simple for regular verbs (work→worked), but irregular verbs have special forms (make→made, grow→grown).

items.sentence: Coffee is grown in many countries.
Cappuccinos are made with steamed milk.
The beans are roasted at high temperatures.
items.translation_ar: تُحمص الحبوب في درجات حرارة عالية

items.sentence: Fair trade coffee is sold in supermarkets.
items.translation_ar: تُباع قهوة التجارة العادلة في السوبر ماركت

items.explanation_ar: الفعل غير النظامي make يصبح made

items.correct: Turkish coffee is prepared with sugar.
